api: Salesflare API 1.0.0 (https://api.salesflare.com/docs; live OpenAPI/Swagger 2.0 spec fetched from https://api.salesflare.com/openapi.json, 2026-07-03)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion against the complete documented Salesflare API v1.0.0 (47 paths, fetched live). 14 read streams: the 3 legacy-parity streams (accounts, contacts, opportunities) plus users, tags, tasks, workflows, groups, stages, pipelines, persons, currencies, custom_field_types, and email_data_sources. 22 write actions: full create/update/delete lifecycle for accounts, contacts, opportunities, tags, and tasks; create/update/delete for meetings; create for calls; create/update/delete for internal notes (messages). Excluded: account-scoped compound sub-resource mutations (contacts/users membership updates, feed, messages) whose real semantics are additive/positional list operations rather than a single-record create/update/delete this dialect can express faithfully; per-item-class custom-field CRUD (would require a config-supplied itemClass path segment per call, not a fixed resource); settings/configuration singletons (AI settings, filter fields); and detail-by-id GETs that duplicate an already-covered list stream's record shape.

excluded.reason: single-account detail; identical record shape to the accounts list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
account-to-contact membership add/link operation, not a single-record create/update/delete on a syncable resource; the dialect's write shapes do not model list-membership mutations
account-to-contact membership replace operation, not a single-record create/update/delete on a syncable resource; the dialect's write shapes do not model list-membership mutations
per-account activity feed/timeline sub-resource; a fan_out read over every account id is beyond this pass's breadth-vs-cost triage
per-account message/internal-note sub-resource; a fan_out read over every account id is beyond this pass's breadth-vs-cost triage
account-to-user assignment add/link operation, not a single-record create/update/delete on a syncable resource; the dialect's write shapes do not model list-membership mutations
account-to-user assignment replace operation, not a single-record create/update/delete on a syncable resource; the dialect's write shapes do not model list-membership mutations
call updates share the same meeting_id-keyed update shape as update_meeting; not modeled as a second write action for the same underlying record type
returns the set of template merge-field tokens available to workflow email campaigns, not a syncable object stream
no list/discovery endpoint exists for conference ids independent of an already-known meeting; a lone detail-by-id GET with no enumeration path is not a syncable stream
single-contact detail; identical record shape to the contacts list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

per-item-class custom-field definition list; itemClass is a caller-supplied path segment (accounts/contacts/opportunities/etc.), not a fixed resource this dialect's static path templating models as one stream
per-item-class custom-field creation; itemClass is a caller-supplied path segment, not a fixed resource
per-custom-field dropdown option list requiring both itemClass and customFieldApiField path segments in hand; not a syncable top-level collection
single-custom-field detail; would duplicate the excluded per-item-class list's record shape
per-item-class custom-field update; itemClass is a caller-supplied path segment, not a fixed resource
deletes a custom-field DEFINITION (schema-level metadata affecting every record of that item class), not a data-record mutation; also requires a caller-supplied itemClass path segment
reconfigures a connected email account's sync settings; an account-security-adjacent mailbox-integration setting, not an ordinary CRM data record mutation
returns the set of fields available for building a saved filter on the given entity type, not a syncable object stream
single-group detail; identical record shape to the groups list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
authenticated-user profile introspection, not a syncable object stream
same contact records as the contacts list stream, filtered to the caller's own assigned contacts; not a distinct resource shape
no top-level list endpoint exists to enumerate meeting ids; a lone detail-by-id GET with no discovery path is not a syncable stream without an id already in hand from a write response
records AI-generated-message quality feedback (thumbs up/down style), not a CRM data-record mutation
identical AI-message-feedback endpoint to POST /message/{message_id}/feedback (singular vs. plural path prefix); not a CRM data-record mutation either way
single-opportunity detail; identical record shape to the opportunities list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
account-wide AI-feature configuration singleton, not a syncable object stream
account-wide AI-feature configuration change, an admin/settings action rather than an ordinary CRM data-record mutation
single-stage detail; identical record shape to the stages list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-tag detail; identical record shape to the tags list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
per-tag usage-count breakdown across workflows/filters/reports; a fan_out read over every tag id is beyond this pass's breadth-vs-cost triage
single-user detail; identical record shape to the users list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

creates a full marketing-automation workflow definition (trigger + multi-step action graph); no documented flat request schema, and workflow authoring is a builder-tool operation rather than a plain CRM record create
single-workflow detail; identical record shape to the workflows list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
updates a full marketing-automation workflow's trigger/step graph; a builder-tool operation, not a plain CRM record update
re-enters or exits a single entity from a running workflow's audience; a workflow-runtime control action, not a CRM data-record mutation
